mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is elevated post-recession (above 8%), it signifies substantial economic slack. A decrease in the unemployment rate from these high levels is a strong positive signal of a healing labor market, boosting consumer confidence and future earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 8.9% as of latest reading, down from a peak over 10%
failure_condition: A 3-month decrease in UNRATE while it is above 8% is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, any acceleration in core inflation signals a potential for premature monetary tightening. This increases the discount rate on future earnings and creates policy uncertainty, negatively impacting S&P 500 valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales and persistently depressed housing starts indicates a consumer-led recovery that is not yet causing broad inflationary pressure from the construction sector. This 'Goldilocks' scenario of growth without overheating is bullish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market remains weak post-2008 crisis
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in industrial production indicates that the supply side of the economy is expanding to meet recovering demand. This translates directly to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive short-term equity performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been recovering steadily since its 2009 trough
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a ZIRP environment, the Fed's primary tool is forward guidance. An unexpected rise in inflation forces the Fed's hand, creating a conflict between its employment and inflation mandates. This policy uncertainty, driven by the interaction of low rates and rising prices, is negative for risk assets.
failure_condition: A model including the interaction term between FEDFUNDS level and CPIAUCSL change does not show a statistically significant negative coefficient for predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls is a high-frequency, direct indicator of economic health. Positive surprises in this series lead to immediate upward revisions of GDP and earnings forecasts, causing positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is recovering but remains well below pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a PAYEMS release that beats consensus expectations by more than 50k is not positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP growth quarter-over-quarter is the canonical signal of a contracting economy. This triggers broad-based reductions in corporate earnings forecasts and investor risk appetite, leading to negative forward returns as the market prices in a recession.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following the release of a negative q/q GDPC1 print, the S&P 500 posts positive returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently weak new housing construction, a key component of investment and durable goods consumption, acts as a drag on aggregate demand. Levels below 750k units signal a lack of confidence and credit availability, negatively impacting forward-looking growth expectations and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest HOUST reading is 596.0, far below historical averages
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 750k, the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically lower than when HOUST is above 750k.
